title: hymenophyllum pilosissimum
intro: Hymenophyllum pilosissimum, also known as the hairy fork fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a small, delicate fern with hairy leaves that is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. Hymenophyllum pilosissimum has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
